machinery_genes.quote: HrcC (= HrpH) is homologous to PulD, pIV, and other members of the outer membrane secretin superfamily of the general secretion pathway.
machinery_genes.note: Deng & Huang 1999 (P. syringae pv. syringae 61). HrcC = former HrpH; shown by cell fractionation and immunogold to be an outer membrane protein required for HrpZ secretion. Same hrp/hrc cluster is conserved in DC3000 (Alfano 2000, Roine 1997 name HrcC/HrpH directly in DC3000).

machinery_genes.role: inner-ring lipoprotein bridging inner and outer membranes
machinery_genes.quote: The hrcJ (= hrpC) gene in the hrpZ operon encodes a putative lipoprotein with extensive similarity to YscJ and MxiJ.
machinery_genes.note: Deng & Huang 1999. HrcJ localized between both membranes; required for HrpZ secretion. YscJ/MxiJ are the canonical SctJ inner-ring lipoproteins (apparatus brief).

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic type III secretion ATPase (energizes export)
machinery_genes.quote: We show that hrcN encodes the putative TTS ATPase of Pseudomonas syringae pathovar phaseolicola and that HrcN is a peripheral protein that assembles in clusters at the membrane.
machinery_genes.note: Pozidis et al 2003 characterise HrcN as the F1/V1-ATPase-homologous secretion ATPase. Paper uses P. syringae pv. phaseolicola, the same Hrp1-family hrp/hrc system; hrcN is also named in DC3000's hrpJ operon (Roine 1997; Rezzonico 2004). SctN/YscN/HrcN ATPase per apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: This hrpJ operon is a 5,651-bp regulation unit, which includes hrpJ, hrcV, hrpQ, and hrcN.
machinery_genes.note: Rezzonico et al 2004 describe the P. syringae hrpJ operon (the same operon organisation as DC3000) containing hrcV. HrcV is the SctV/InvA/LcrD-family central export-apparatus component (apparatus brief). Role assigned from SctV family vocabulary.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane (MS-ring) component of the secretion apparatus
machinery_genes.quote: This hrpJ operon is a 5,651-bp regulation unit, which includes hrpJ, hrcV, hrpQ, and hrcN.
machinery_genes.note: Rezzonico et al 2004; hrpQ is co-transcribed in the hrpJ secretion operon between hrcV and hrcN. HrpQ corresponds to the SctD/HrpQ inner-MS-ring component (apparatus brief lists HrpQ as the plant-Hrp SctD synonym). Lower-confidence family assignment (named only as an operon member, not functionally dissected here).

machinery_genes.quote: The hrpJ and hrpU operons contain seven hrc genes (counting hrcQ(A) and hrcQ(B) as one gene), all with additional homologs involved in flagellar biogenesis and secretion, and five of which encode predicted inner membrane proteins.
machinery_genes.note: Charkowski et al 1997 (Pss61). HrcQ is split into hrcQ(A) and hrcQ(B); SctQ-family cytoplasmic C-ring/sorting-platform (apparatus brief: HrcQ = SctQ in plant-Hrp). Required-for-secretion phenotype shown for the hrpJ/hrpU operon hrc genes in the same paper.

machinery_genes.role: Hrp pilus major structural subunit (extracellular needle/pilus)
machinery_genes.quote: Furthermore, we have identified HrpA as a major structural protein of the Hrp pilus.
machinery_genes.note: Roine et al 1997, directly in P. syringae pv. tomato DC3000. A nonpolar hrpA mutant cannot form the Hrp pilus or cause HR/disease. The Hrp pilus is the plant-pathogen analogue of the SctF needle (apparatus brief: HrpA = Hrp pilus subunit).

machinery_genes.quote: The hrpJ and hrpU operons ... Inner membrane translocator components
machinery_genes.note: Collmer et al 2000 Table 1: the hrpJ and hrpU operons encode inner-membrane translocator/secretion components; hrpJ heads the secretion (hrpJ) operon (Rezzonico 2004). HrpJ is the P. syringae SctW-class gatekeeper (apparatus brief: HrpJ = gatekeeper). Family label kept descriptive because the verbatim quote names it as an inner-membrane translocator operon rather than spelling out the gatekeeper role.

machinery_genes.role: putative translocator at the apparatus border (HrpK1 translocon-associated)
machinery_genes.quote: The EELs begin 3 nt downstream of the stop codon of hrpK
machinery_genes.note: Alfano et al 2000; hrpK is the terminal gene of the conserved hrp/hrc cluster, marking the cluster boundary before the exchangeable effector locus. HrpK (HrpK1) is later characterised as a translocon-class protein (apparatus brief lists HrpF/HrpK as the plant-Hrp translocon). Included as apparatus, not effector; quote here names hrpK as the cluster-boundary gene. Lower confidence as a pore component from this quote alone.

notes: System = the Hrp1 (group I) Hrp/Hrc type III secretion system of P. syringae pv. tomato DC3000, encoded in the conserved hrp/hrc gene cluster of the tripartite Hrp pathogenicity island (Alfano 2000, Collmer 2000). The cluster is essentially identical across P. syringae pv. syringae 61 (Pss61), pv. syringae B728a, and pv. tomato DC3000, so apparatus papers on Pss61 and pv. phaseolicola transfer directly to DC3000; the founding Hrp-pilus and HrpH/HrcC work was done directly in DC3000 (Roine 1997). Nine conserved apparatus genes are designated hrc (Bogdanove 1996; Charkowski 1997 abstract: 'The nine hrc genes represent a subset of hrp genes ... conserved in the type III virulence protein secretion systems of animal pathogenic Yersinia, Shigella, and Salmonella'). I list only genes I could anchor to a verbatim naming quote in literature I could actually read: HrcC (OM secretin), HrcJ (SctJ lipoprotein), HrcN (ATPase), HrcV (SctV export ring), HrpQ (SctD, hrpJ-operon member), HrcQ (SctQ C-ring; hrcQ(A)+hrcQ(B)), HrpA (Hrp pilus/SctF), HrpJ (gatekeeper/translocator-control), HrpK (translocon/border). DELIBERATELY NOT LISTED despite being part of the canonical nine-hrc set, because the only paper that names each individually (Bogdanove 1996 unified-nomenclature letter, PMID 8736546) is paywalled and I could not retrieve a verbatim per-gene quote from an accessible source: HrcR, HrcS, HrcT (small inner-membrane export-gate genes, SctR/SctS/SctT, in the hrpU operon) and HrcU (SctU export-gate autoprotease, hrpU operon). These four are well established in the literature as members of the P. syringae hrpU operon (Charkowski 1997 states the hrpJ+hrpU operons carry seven hrc genes), so the apparatus is real but my per-gene quote coverage is incomplete -> status PARTIAL. Status is PARTIAL rather than COMPLETE specifically because HrcR/S/T/U lack an extractable naming quote. Also excluded as non-machinery: secreted effectors/Avr/Hop proteins (the 25 curated effectors and HopPsyA/AvrPto), the secreted harpins HrpZ and HrpW and the harpin/translocator HrpW (secreted accessory, not core injectisome), escort chaperones (Shc*), and regulators HrpL (ECF sigma), HrpR/HrpS (NtrC-family activators) and HrpV (negative regulator) per the apparatus brief. Lowest-confidence machinery calls: HrpQ and HrpK (named via operon membership / cluster-boundary quotes rather than direct functional dissection in the quoted sentence). All quotes are verbatim from PubMed-indexed full texts/abstracts; all PMIDs and DOIs verified via get_article_metadata.
